contentBody: Why do developers use Version Control Systems?
mcscAnswer: To track changes and restore older versions
option.2: To track changes and restore older versions
answerExplanation: Version Control Systems help track code changes and allow restoring previous versions when needed.

contentBody: How does Version Control help in team collaboration?
mcscAnswer: Prevents overwriting others’ work and manages conflicts
option.3: Prevents overwriting others’ work and manages conflicts
answerExplanation: It allows multiple developers to work safely by handling conflicts and tracking contributions.

contentBody: Which of the following is a Distributed Version Control System (DVCS)?
answerExplanation: Git is a distributed system where every developer has a complete repository copy.

contentBody: What is Git mainly used for?
mcscAnswer: Tracking changes and managing versions locally
option.2: Tracking changes and managing versions locally
answerExplanation: Git is used for local version control and tracking code changes.

contentBody: What additional capability does GitHub provide over Git?
mcscAnswer: Cloud-based collaboration like pull requests & code reviews
option.2: Cloud-based collaboration like pull requests & code reviews
answerExplanation: GitHub enables collaboration features like PRs, reviews, and remote repositories.

contentBody: Which Git area stores the permanent snapshots of your project?
answerExplanation: The local repository stores all committed project history permanently.

contentBody: Why is the Staging Area important in Git?
mcscAnswer: It helps review and choose what goes into the next commit
option.3: It helps review and choose what goes into the next commit
option.4: It replaces the need for commit messages
answerExplanation: The staging area allows selecting specific changes before committing.

contentBody: Which command is used to unstage a file without removing its modifications?
answerExplanation: This command removes the file from staging but keeps the changes in the working directory.
